message:
feat: enforce closed_at consistency in issue validation

Two new validation rules are added to IssueValidator:

1. Closed issues (status == Closed) must have `closed_at` set.
   Previously a closed issue with `closed_at: None` would pass
   validation, creating an inconsistent record with no closure
   timestamp.

2. Non-terminal issues (anything other than Closed or Tombstone)
   must NOT have `closed_at` set. An open or in-progress issue
   carrying a `closed_at` timestamp is contradictory and now
   produces a validation error.

Tombstone issues are explicitly exempted from rule 1 — they
represent deletions and may or may not carry a closure timestamp
depending on whether the issue was closed before being tombstoned.

The proptest `all_standard_statuses_valid` test is updated to set
`closed_at` when generating a Closed status, so the fuzz harness
remains valid under the new rules.

Unit tests cover: closed-without-closed_at (rejected),
non-terminal-with-closed_at (rejected), and tombstone-without-
closed_at (accepted).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
